raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes repairing the contribution framing and thesis because the artifact makes conflicting claims about being end-to-end versus pipeline-based.","R1.2":"Logical consistency is load-bearing because the draft contains direct contradictions between the claimed architecture, training setup, and later explanation of gains.","R1.3":"Evidence-claim alignment is central because performance claims and causal explanations need to match the reported training procedure and evaluation results.","R1.5":"Structural reorganization is central because improving the draft may require reordering or reframing sections so the method, results, and contribution narrative cohere.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation explicitly asks to improve a draft, which normally includes sentence-level clarity, flow, and register.","RX.1":"Uncertainty disclosure is active because the projection must preserve uncertainty about unstated author intent and unsupported claims.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is active because the artifact includes strong firstness and generalization claims that may exceed the presented evidence.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is active because the task is only to project responsibilities, not to edit or evaluate the paper itself.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is active because misleading scientific claims or unsupported novelty assertions could affect downstream readers and reviewers.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability is active because responsibility assignments should be traceable to the delegation wording and specific features of the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes repairing the contribution framing and thesis because the artifact makes conflicting claims about being end-to-end versus pipeline-based.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is load-bearing because the draft contains direct contradictions between the claimed architecture, training setup, and later explanation of gains.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is central because performance claims and causal explanations need to match the reported training procedure and evaluation results.
rationale.R1.5: Structural reorganization is central because improving the draft may require reordering or reframing sections so the method, results, and contribution narrative cohere.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation explicitly asks to improve a draft, which normally includes sentence-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is active because the projection must preserve uncertainty about unstated author intent and unsupported claims.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is active because the artifact includes strong firstness and generalization claims that may exceed the presented evidence.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is active because the task is only to project responsibilities, not to edit or evaluate the paper itself.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is active because misleading scientific claims or unsupported novelty assertions could affect downstream readers and reviewers.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is active because responsibility assignments should be traceable to the delegation wording and specific features of the artifact.
